name: Nichols Bus
principals: Robyn Nichols (President); Jim Nichols (prior owner, deceased 2018); founded by Lyle 'Shorty' Nichols; 4th generation (S & K Nichols) involved
recent_news: Third-generation family business; Jim Nichols (2nd-gen owner) passed in 2018, daughter Robyn took over as President; retired motorcoach line in 2010 to focus on school transportation.
fit_notes: Small NEPA family operator (~12 buses) anchored to Lackawanna Trail SD. Third-gen leadership with 4th-gen involvement suggests some runway, but small scale and rural footprint = modest tuck-in for Summit's PA cluster; succession optionality is medium given owner-operator structure.

name: Kip's Bus Svc
principals: Mary Clyde and Jan Gourley (owners per BBB); lead exec listed as Jan Rowles (likely same Jan); operates as Kips Bus Service Inc / Kips CDL Testing
recent_news: In business since 1967 (~59 years); also operates Kips CDL Testing Inc, partnering with Curwensville SD to train substitute bus drivers amid driver shortages (WTAJ coverage).
fit_notes: ~20-bus single-district contractor (Curwensville Area SD) in rural Clearfield County PA, ~60-year-old family business with female owner-operators – high succession probability. Small but clean tuck-in; CDL testing arm is a bonus driver-pipeline asset for Summit's PA platform.

name: Schwartz School Bus Svc
principals: Jerry Schwartz (President, Schwartz Bus Service Inc)
districts_or_customers: Montgomery County MO area schools (Jonesburg/Montgomery City area); specific district contracts unconfirmed
recent_news: Received $104,370 PPP loan in April 2020 reporting 40 jobs in school/employee bus transport; in business ~43 years (since 1982).
website: unconfirmed (no dedicated company website found; directory listings only)
fit_notes: Mid-Missouri small family operator (~20 buses, 40 employees per PPP) – not in Summit's core PA/MA/etc. footprint, but a clean independent. Founder-led since 1982 suggests succession timing is reasonable; geographic fit is the main question for Summit's tuck-in strategy.

name: Boyarsky Bus Co LLC
principals: Unconfirmed; Boyarsky family of Scott Township/Lackawanna County PA (Charles Leo Boyarsky d. 2003 buried in Scott Twp may be family namesake)
districts_or_customers: likely Lackawanna Trail SD or other Lackawanna County PA districts (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No web presence located; listed website (Crossfitkanna.Com) is unrelated (CrossFit gym), suggesting the company has no real online footprint or is inactive/dormant; no FMCSA/PA PUC record surfaced under that exact name.
website: none confirmed; data-record website (crossfitkanna.com) is unrelated
fit_notes: Very low confidence target – essentially zero verifiable web/regulatory footprint under this exact name. Could be a micro/owner-operator, dormant LLC, or a record-keeping artifact. Requires phone outreach or PA PUC lookup before pursuing; not a priority tuck-in until verified as an active operator.

name: Mario's Transportation Inc
principals: Mario Lopez Sr. (founder); Mario Lopez (President, per record); family-owned and operated
districts_or_customers: NYC private schools: Chapin, Collegiate, Trinity, Columbia Grammar, Brearley
recent_news: 33-year-old family business; grew from single commuter shuttle (Upper East Side to FiDi) to 30+ vehicle fleet; serves elite Manhattan private K-12 schools; modern fleet (2007+ with seatbelts, AC).
fit_notes: Attractive niche operator: contracts with top-tier NYC private schools (Chapin, Trinity, Brearley, Collegiate, Columbia Grammar) = premium private-pay revenue with low district-procurement risk. ~30 buses, founder-led from 1991 (succession window opening). Strong tuck-in candidate for Summit's NYC private-school exposure if Summit expands beyond traditional yellow-bus district contracts.
